nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and outer-reef habitats, typically encountered at depths of roughly 0–25 m, usually solitary or in pairs

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding on benthic invertebrates and zooplankton. In captivity is generally fed a varied diet of meaty marine fare such as finely chopped marine fish, crustacean meats, mysis shrimp, and vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, complemented by algae-based foods. Feeding two or more times daily is commonly recommended, and vitamin supplementation has been reported to help maintain coloration.

summary: A rarely-imported South Pacific butterflyfish endemic to French Polynesia, with a grey-and-brown two-tone body and a dark vertical eye bar. Generally considered intermediate in difficulty and unsuitable for reef aquaria.
careNotes:
The Tahitian Butterflyfish is endemic to French Polynesia (Society, Tahiti, and Tuamotu Islands) and is only rarely available in the marine aquarium trade. It is generally considered a peaceful species that tends to do well in a mature FOWLR aquarium with non-aggressive tankmates, though it has been reported to become territorial if housed in too small a system.

It is not considered reef-safe — sources note that it may pick at coral polyps and small ornamental invertebrates, consistent with the Chaetodon genus' wild diet of benthic invertebrates and zooplankton.

This species is best kept singly or as a mated pair; pairs are generally advised to be introduced to the aquarium simultaneously to reduce aggression.

Minimum tank size recommendations vary across sources: LiveAquaria and fishy.wiki list 70 US gallons (~265 L), Quality Marine lists 55 gallons (~208 L), and at least one community source recommends 600 L for long-term care of an active 12-cm butterflyfish that needs ample swimming space. An averaged but rounded value of 300 L is used here, biased upward to account for the species' need for swimming room and the systematic under-statement of tank sizes by retail sources.

Water quality should be kept stable; like other butterflyfishes, it can be susceptible to HLLE-style symptoms when kept in poor conditions or fed an insufficiently varied diet, so vitamin-enriched and algae-based foods are generally advised alongside meaty fare.

Captive breeding has not been reliably documented. Like other Chaetodon species, it is reported to be oviparous and to form pairs during spawning.
breedingNotes: Reported to be oviparous and to form pairs during the breeding season; spawning has been documented in the wild but the species has not been reliably bred in captivity under typical hobby conditions.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (12.0 cm TL), distribution (Society Islands, Tahiti, Tuamotu Islands), depth range (0–25 m), taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), and reproduction (oviparous, pairs).

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperament, diet, reef safety and water parameters. Tank size listed as 55 gallons (~208 L) vs LiveAquaria's 70 gallons; averaged and biased upward to 300 L per tank-size special rule.

additional.name: fishy.wiki
additional.notes: Third cross-source for water parameters and adult size (12.7 cm). Recommends 264 L minimum and notes the species requires ample swimming space.

fish.breedingNotes: Reported oviparous and to form pairs during spawning; not reliably bred in captivity under typical hobby conditions.
